title: Treatise on Garsing
summary: The checked-in XML is the pinned repository representation of University of Michigan Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse item CME302, a raw transcript made by Paul F. Schaffner in May 2020 and marked up in December 2020. It transcribes only Hunter 307, f. 166r line 26-f. 166v: the final garsing and ventosing component of the larger Middle English On Bloodletting beginning at f. 165v line 14. A 2023 article supplies a later semi-diplomatic edition, codicology, and dialect analysis, while three separately modeled plates reproduce ff. 165v-166v; no derivation from Schaffner's transcript is evidenced. WebHunter's JPEG facsimile and María Laura Esteban Segura's diplomatic transcription are also separate digital layers. Its first two viewer sequences overlap by 98 slots, and its third repeats full-folio images across left/right transcription slots, so published slot counts are not unique physical surfaces. CME302 maps to hu307p2 JPEGs pag99-c.jpg-pag100-c.jpg and text files page98.txt-page99.txt. Explicit WebHunter image terms prohibit copying without prior written consent, so no image bundle was cached. The complete scholarly article, public-domain 1908 Hunterian catalog, and current British Library digitisation price list were cached. The U-M XML also identifies collateral versions in Royal MS 18 A VI, Sloane MS 965, and Sloane MS 3486; these are supporting witnesses cited by the encoding, not ancestors of CME302. No public complete image surrogate was found in their current British Library records, but exact reproduction-order and permissions routes are recorded.
review_notes: CME302 is scoped to the final line of f. 166r and f. 166v, not to the complete ff. 165v-166v On Bloodletting text.
Voigts's treatment of the Hunter passage within VK 0659.00 and of the three parallel passages as VK 1841.00 is preserved as a repertory-boundary problem rather than silently resolved.

The first 98 WebHunter hu307p2 viewer slots duplicate hu307p1 surfaces from f. 115r through f. 165v, and hu307p3 repeats full-folio JPEGs across paired left/right transcription slots; provider numpages values are viewer slots, not unique images or physical surfaces.

WebHunter's description says f. 161 is missing, but its own viewer exposes f. 161r-v; Glasgow's current catalog instead reports missing ff. 147-148. The conflict is retained rather than silently reconciled.

The current University of Glasgow IIIF catalog manifest has only two zero-overlap sample canvases. Its license field points to the Glasgow home page rather than a reuse license, and current publication guidance requires advance permission, so the samples remain catalog evidence and were not separately cached or modeled as a reusable facsimile.

entities.label: General Corpus repository artifact for CME302
entities.description: The exact selected XML used by General Corpus. Duplicate source resolution is an explicit publication override because this path is printed as the source XML in the approved PDF workflow.
notes: The XML identifies itself as CME302 and also carries IDG, BIBNO, and VID value CME00302.

entities.label: University of Michigan CME item CME302
entities.description: A public U-M Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse digital text derived directly from Hunter 307, ff. 166r-v. The repository XML says Schaffner transcribed it in May 2020, added markup in December 2020, and created the header in February 2023.

entities.label: Glasgow University Library, MS Hunter 307 (U.7.1), ff. 166r-v
entities.description: The direct manuscript witness of CME302. The cataloged component begins at f. 166r line 26 and ends on f. 166v; the larger On Bloodletting text begins at f. 165v line 14. The codex is an early-fifteenth-century English medical volume, while its current catalog also preserves older fourteenth-century and original-binding descriptions alongside A. I. Doyle's later corrections.

date_statements.label: Preferred current scholarly date
date_statements.notes: The current catalog retains the legacy Cent. XIV description but separately records Doyle's early-fifteenth-century hand assessment.

holding.notes: Archives and Special Collections, University of Glasgow. The full institutional reference is GB 247 MS Hunter 307 (U.7.1).

physical_description.format: Bound codex; one column of 26 lines
physical_description.dimensions: Binding 200 x 140 mm; folios about 190 x 130 mm; written space about 147-150 x 90-95 mm
notes: Black ink with alternating blue and red paragraph marks; gilt and colored initials elsewhere in the codex.
The ff. 165v-166v text is in a careful Gothic textura book hand.
The seventeenth-century marginal note 'Anthony July 13' appears on f. 166v.

entities.label: WebHunter JPEG image sequences for MS Hunter 307
entities.description: The Málaga Corpus WebHunter project publicly presents Hunter 307 JPEGs in three editorial viewer sequences. Provider count files report 329 hu307p1 slots, 100 hu307p2 slots, and 38 hu307p3 slots, but these are viewer/transcription positions rather than unique image objects or physical surfaces. The first 98 hu307p2 slots duplicate hu307p1 from f. 115r through f. 165v; only hu307p2 pag99-c.jpg and pag100-c.jpg add ff. 166r-v. In hu307p3, slots 0-21 are eleven left/right transcription pairs whose two slots reuse one byte-identical full-folio JPEG; slot 22 is f. 172v left, and slots 23-37 are distinct blank or end surfaces. CME302 is on pag99-c.jpg and pag100-c.jpg.
entities.reproduction_format: Public HTML viewer with JPEG manuscript images
notes: No local image bundle was created because the provider's specific copyright page says the facsimile images are all rights reserved and may not be copied or used without prior written consent.

The site-wide image credit groups Glasgow University Library and the Wellcome Library; Hunter 307 is held by Glasgow, but WebHunter does not separately itemize exact image-rights allocation for this manuscript.
WebHunter's description says the codex lacks f. 161, while the viewer exposes f. 161r-v and Glasgow's current catalog reports missing ff. 147-148.

entities.label: WebHunter diplomatic transcription of MS Hunter 307
entities.description: A diplomatic WebHunter transcription aligned to the project's editorial viewer slots and credited on the copyright page to María Laura Esteban Segura. CME302 appears in hu307p2 text files page98.txt and page99.txt, beginning at f. 166r line 26 and continuing through f. 166v. This text layer is distinct from the JPEG facsimile and from Schaffner's U-M transcription.
notes: The broad CC BY-NC-SA footer and the site's more restrictive specific copyright wording require clarification before reuse.

entities.label: Esteban-Segura and Soriano-Jiménez 2023 edition and study of On Bloodletting
entities.description: A peer-reviewed study and semi-diplomatic edition of the larger Hunter 307 text on ff. 165v-166v. The CME302 portion begins at f. 166r line 26 on printed p. 117 and continues on p. 119. The editors describe direct consultation of the manuscript or its digital images; the article does not evidence derivation from Schaffner's 2020 transcript.

bibliographic.citation: Laura Esteban-Segura and Carlos Soriano-Jiménez, 'Guy de Chauliac's On Bloodletting in Glasgow, University Library, MS Hunter 307 (ff. 165v-166v),' Revista Canaria de Estudios Ingleses 87 (2023): 107-130
bibliographic.title: Guy de Chauliac's On Bloodletting in Glasgow, University Library, MS Hunter 307 (ff. 165v-166v)

entities.label: Manuscript plates in Esteban-Segura and Soriano-Jiménez 2023
entities.description: Three printed manuscript plates embedded in the article reproduce Hunter 307, ff. 165v-166v. Figure 13 on p. 114 shows f. 165v and is outside CME302; Figures 14-15 on pp. 116 and 118 show ff. 166r-v and overlap CME302. This is a complete capture of the article's selected plates, not a complete manuscript facsimile.
entities.reproduction_format: Three color manuscript plates embedded in the journal PDF
notes: The article says the manuscript images are reproduced by kind permission of Glasgow University Library; its CC BY-NC-ND statement is not assumed to license these third-party plates separately.

entities.label: Young and Aitken 1908 Hunterian catalog description of MS 307
entities.description: The detailed historical printed catalog record cited by the current Glasgow catalog. It supplies collation, dimensions, decoration, condition, contents, and former shelfmarks but assigns a fourteenth-century date and original binding that the current record also qualifies with Doyle's later assessment.

bibliographic.citation: John Young and P. Henderson Aitken, A Catalogue of the Manuscripts in the Library of the Hunterian Museum in the University of Glasgow (Glasgow: James Maclehose and Sons, 1908), 245-246
bibliographic.title: A Catalogue of the Manuscripts in the Library of the Hunterian Museum in the University of Glasgow

entities.label: British Library, Royal MS 18 A VI, ff. 55r-55v
entities.description: A collateral Middle English version of the garsing and ventosing passage. The current British Library catalog describes Guy de Chauliac on hematoscopy, scarifying, and cupping on ff. 54r-55v; the U-M XML scopes the parallel component to ff. 55r-55v.

date_statements.label: Current catalog date range for codex
date_statements.notes: This broad codex date is not automatically a composition date for the parallel component.

date_statements.label: Relevant component hand
date_statements.notes: The detailed contents/related-material description identifies the Guy de Chauliac item as article 3 in another late-fifteenth-century hand; this component date is kept separate from the broader codex range.

notes: Current catalog title: Medical treatises, chiefly in Middle English.

entities.label: British Library, Sloane MS 965, ff. 163v-164v
entities.description: A collateral Middle English version of the garsing and ventosing passage, identified and scoped by the U-M XML within a codex whose current catalog lists fourteenth- to fifteenth-century English bloodletting tracts on ff. 156v-164v.

date_statements.label: Relevant bloodletting section
date_statements.notes: This date applies to the English bloodletting tracts on ff. 156v-164v, including the cited ff. 163v-164v parallel, not to the 1700-1701 seed-list component used as the record's top-level date.

notes: The current record's top-level 1700-1701 date describes a Hampton Court seed-list component and must not be transferred to the medieval medical contents; the contents list dates the bloodletting tracts fourteenth-fifteenth century.

entities.label: British Library, Sloane MS 3486, ff. 147v-148
entities.description: A collateral Middle English version of the garsing and ventosing passage. The U-M XML literally gives ff. 147v-48, normalized only to ff. 147v-148 with the terminal side unresolved; the 2023 study identifies f. 147v and dates this version to the second or third quarter of the fifteenth century.

date_statements.label: Date of parallel version
date_statements.value: Second or third quarter of the fifteenth century
date_statements.notes: The current catalog's thirteenth-seventeenth-century range applies to the composite codex, not this exact component.

notes: No direct manuscript or eVK2 evidence reviewed here resolves whether the XML's terminal f. 148 means recto, verso, or an unspecified part of the folio.

access.access_method: Read the pinned XML file in the General Corpus checkout.
access.format: XML

access.access_method: Open the U-M CME item. Automated requests encountered a Cloudflare challenge during review, but the stable item identifier and pinned source XML remain available.
access.format: HTML digital text and XML-derived display

access.access_method: Read the exact current Glasgow collection record and its two sample canvases.
access.format: IIIF Presentation 2 metadata record with two sample images
notes: The two canvases show William Hunter's inscription and an opening text page; they have zero overlap with ff. 166r-v and are not a complete codex surrogate.

The manifest's license field points to the Glasgow home page rather than a recognized reuse license, while current Glasgow publication guidance requires advance permission. The sample images were therefore not separately cached or modeled as a reusable facsimile.

access.access_method: Email Archives and Special Collections for advice and an appointment; the item record says access is open but an appointment may be required.
access.format: Onsite manuscript consultation

access.access_method: Email Archives and Special Collections with the shelfmark and a targeted research question. The current service can share up to 20 quick-reference snaps and can advise on fuller imaging or a virtual reading-room appointment.
access.format: Email enquiry and limited non-publication reference images
notes: The free enquiry service advertises up to 20 quick snaps, not publication-standard photography or a complete codex facsimile; ask separately about whole-manuscript imaging.

access.access_method: Use Glasgow's publishing-request instructions and contact Archives and Special Collections before publishing manuscript images. Permission, acknowledgement, fees, and separate copyright clearance may apply.
access.format: Online instructions and application route

access.access_method: Open WebHunter, choose Hunter 307, and use its three manuscript sections. CME302 is in the second section at f. 166r-v, JPEGs pag99-c.jpg and pag100-c.jpg. Deep Angular routes may return an error on direct reload, so begin at the home page if necessary.
access.format: HTML viewer and JPEG images
notes: No local copy is recorded. The provider's exact copyright language prohibits copying the images without prior written consent, so systematic extraction was not performed.

The first 98 hu307p2 slots duplicate hu307p1 surfaces, and hu307p3 also assigns one full-folio JPEG to separate left/right transcription slots. Provider numpages counts must not be appended or summed as unique images.

access.access_method: Open WebHunter's Hunter 307 second section or the exact text files. CME302 begins at line 26 in page98.txt and continues through page99.txt.

notes: No local copy is recorded because WebHunter's specific copyright page says site information cannot be republished, modified, or copied without permission, despite the separate CC BY-NC-SA footer.

The online service distinguishes reference images from higher-quality reproduction images and says original manuscripts are photographed rather than scanned. Ordering and payment do not themselves grant repository publication or commercial reuse rights.

rights.component: CME302 transcript and markup as represented in the pinned XML
rights.basis: The exact U-M XML says the materials are in the public domain and neither the University nor the editor claims copyright in them.
notes: This assertion does not license WebHunter images, the 2023 article, or British Library manuscript photography.

rights.component: U-M CME302 transcript and encoded digital text
rights.basis: The item-level XML availability statement declares the materials public domain and disclaims copyright claims by U-M and the editor.

rights.component: Underlying medieval Middle English text on ff. 166r-v
rights.basis: The text is anonymously translated and attested in an early-fifteenth-century manuscript; this conclusion is limited to the historical text and not modern photography, transcription, or editorial matter.

rights.component: WebHunter facsimile images of MS Hunter 307
rights.jurisdiction: Provider terms and applicable copyright law
rights.basis: The WebHunter copyright page gives a site-wide facsimile-image credit to Glasgow University Library and the Wellcome Library, says all rights are reserved, and requires prior written consent to use images or pictures. Hunter 307 is held by Glasgow, but the page does not separately allocate rights for this exact image sequence.
notes: Public viewing is not redistribution permission. No systematic local cache was made.
Contact Glasgow Archives and Special Collections and the WebHunter rights holders before publishing or bulk copying these images.

rights.component: WebHunter site information and manuscript transcriptions
rights.basis: The site footer applies CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 to the Málaga Corpus, while its specific copyright page says the website and information cannot be republished, modified, or copied without permission and credits the Hunter 307 transcription to María Laura Esteban Segura.
notes: The broad footer license and the more restrictive specific copyright wording conflict in practical scope. Obtain clarification before copying or republishing the transcription.

rights.component: Authorial study, semi-diplomatic edition, and publisher PDF, excluding third-party manuscript plates
rights.basis: The journal article page applies Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial-NoDerivatives 4.0 International.
notes: The CC statement is scoped here to the article's authorial and editorial content and its distributed carrier. The separately modeled manuscript plates are excluded because the article says they were reproduced by permission of Glasgow University Library.

rights.jurisdiction: Provider terms and applicable copyright law
rights.basis: The article states that all manuscript images are reproduced by kind permission of Glasgow University Library. No item-level statement extends the article's CC BY-NC-ND license to these third-party photographs.
notes: Public access to the article PDF does not establish permission to extract, republish, or redistribute the manuscript plates separately.

rights.basis: The catalog was published in 1908, before the current United States public-domain cutoff.
notes: No broader territorial conclusion is asserted from publication date alone.

rights.component: Prospective British Library manuscript reproductions of Royal MS 18 A VI
rights.basis: No public complete image object or item-level image license was found. British Library Imaging Services terms require separate written permission before supplied images are copied, reproduced, published, or distributed; ordering and payment do not themselves grant reuse rights.

rights.component: Prospective British Library manuscript reproductions of Sloane MS 965
rights.basis: No public complete image object or item-level image license was found. British Library Imaging Services terms require separate written permission before supplied images are copied, reproduced, published, or distributed; ordering and payment do not themselves grant reuse rights.

rights.component: Prospective British Library manuscript reproductions of Sloane MS 3486
rights.basis: No public complete image object or item-level image license was found. British Library Imaging Services terms require separate written permission before supplied images are copied, reproduced, published, or distributed; ordering and payment do not themselves grant reuse rights.

editorial_practices.source_feature: The larger On Bloodletting text on Hunter 307, ff. 165v-166v
editorial_practices.representation: Only the final garsing and ventosing component, f. 166r line 26-f. 166v
editorial_practices.description: CME302 begins after the preceding hematoscopy and bloodletting discussion on f. 166r and encodes only the final component conventionally titled Treatise on Garsing.
editorial_practices.scope: The complete U-M CME302 encoding and checked-in repository XML

editorial_practices.source_feature: Manuscript suspension marks, superior letters, brevigraphs, and the special sign for 'and'
editorial_practices.representation: Expanded alphabetic forms without a systematic body-text typographic distinction for supplied letters
editorial_practices.description: Comparison with the WebHunter transcription and the article's palaeographic account shows that manuscript abbreviations are expanded in the U-M body text. The XML does not consistently preserve the italic expansion marking visible in WebHunter's transcription.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Alternating red and blue paragraph marks in the manuscript
editorial_practices.representation: Uniform paragraph-sign characters in plain XML text
editorial_practices.description: The U-M transcription retains paragraph signs but does not encode the manuscript's alternating red and blue colors. WebHunter's transcription uses colored HTML spans, and the 2023 study documents the systematic alternation.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, Treatise on garsing, CME302 XML, transcribed by Paul F. Schaffner
evidence.summary: Direct evidence for item identity, source witness and range, title, transcript dates, U-M publication, public-domain statement, Voigts/eVK2 classifications, three collateral witnesses, prose content, and the encoding's editorial framing.

evidence.citation: General Corpus source-resolution audit for CME302 at base commit 687c2bb
evidence.summary: The reviewed publication-set override selects additions_2023/CME302.xml over CME_additions/CME302.xml because the selected path is printed in the approved PDF colophon. The source entry fixes SHA-256 70925f70e276b83eba7ab294ff497d107c07e12e841c6be89381708b02b2e51f; repository inspection additionally reported 5,741 bytes, git blob e56b96d0dd08da5a94ff2a6d01b753f9c9adc07f, and CME commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Digital Collections, CME302
evidence.summary: Stable public item identifier for the U-M digital text. Automated retrieval encountered a Cloudflare challenge during the review, so exact content claims rely on the pinned U-M XML rather than the challenged interface.

evidence.citation: University of Glasgow Archives and Special Collections, GB 247 MS Hunter 307 (U.7.1), item record 296678
evidence.summary: Current institutional record for shelfmark, holding, physical description, provenance, access contact, older catalog claims, Doyle's hand and binding corrections, publication notes, and two zero-overlap sample canvases. Its license field points to the Glasgow home page rather than a reusable license.

evidence.citation: University of Glasgow Archives and Special Collections, Enquiries service
evidence.summary: The current general-enquiry route invites item-specific research questions, offers targeted research and up to 20 free quick-reference snaps, and distinguishes those images from publication-standard photography and permission-to-publish requests.

evidence.citation: The Málaga Corpus of Late Middle English Scientific Prose, WebHunter description of Hunter 307
evidence.summary: Official project and University of Glasgow routes identify Hunter 307 as a digitized manuscript presented by WebHunter and divide it into three manuscript sections. WebHunter credits the Hunter 307 description and transcription to María Laura Esteban Segura. Its description says the codex lacks ff. 20-21, 147-148, and 161, but the same viewer exposes f. 161r-v and Glasgow's current record instead reports missing ff. 147-148. Direct deep-route reloads may fail, but navigation from the project home page remains the public route.

evidence.citation: WebHunter Hunter 307 viewer-slot count manifests and audited image mappings for hu307p1, hu307p2, and hu307p3
evidence.summary: The exact provider JSON files contain only numpages values 329, 100, and 38, which represent viewer/transcription slots rather than unique image objects. Byte comparison shows hu307p2 slots 0-97 duplicate hu307p1 from f. 115r through f. 165v; only p2 slots 98-99 add ff. 166r-v. In hu307p3, slots 0-21 form eleven left/right transcription pairs with byte-identical JPEGs within each pair; slot 22 is f. 172v left, and slots 23-37 are distinct blank or end surfaces. The counts therefore cannot be summed as 467 unique images or physical surfaces.

evidence.citation: WebHunter Hunter 307, hu307p2 images and transcriptions for ff. 166r-v
evidence.summary: Exact provider JPEGs and separate text files verify that the garsing incipit begins at line 26 on pag99-c.jpg/f. 166r and page98.txt, then continues through pag100-c.jpg/f. 166v and page99.txt. The transcription exposes expansion marking and colored paragraph signs absent from the plain U-M body text.

evidence.citation: The Málaga Corpus of Late Middle English Scientific Prose, Copyright page and current site footer
evidence.summary: The current application text gives a site-wide image credit to Glasgow University Library and the Wellcome Library, says facsimile images are all rights reserved and cannot be used without prior written consent, and says website information cannot be republished, modified, or copied without permission. It credits the Hunter 307 transcription exactly to María Laura Esteban Segura. The footer separately labels the Málaga Corpus CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 and directs permissions beyond that license to the Copyright page.
notes: The direct Copyright deep route returned an HTTP 500 during scripted access, but the exact current text is embedded in the live application's versioned JavaScript and is reachable by in-app navigation.

evidence.citation: Laura Esteban-Segura and Carlos Soriano-Jiménez, 'Guy de Chauliac's On Bloodletting in Glasgow, University Library, MS Hunter 307 (ff. 165v-166v),' Revista Canaria de Estudios Ingleses 87 (2023): 107-130
evidence.locator: especially pp. 109-123; plates pp. 114, 116, 118; edition pp. 115, 117, 119
evidence.summary: Peer-reviewed identification of the larger text as Guy de Chauliac's On Bloodletting, with transmission discussion, codicology, palaeography, anonymous scribe-translator statement, semi-diplomatic edition, exact CME component boundary, Sloane 3486 parallel, and tentative Huntingdonshire localization. The running issue line gives October 2023, while the landing page records online publication on 2023-11-11 and applies CC BY-NC-ND 4.0. The article includes English and Spanish abstracts plus Middle English edition text and says its manuscript plates were reproduced by kind permission of Glasgow University Library.

evidence.citation: Local inspection and text extraction of the complete provider PDF for Esteban-Segura and Soriano-Jiménez 2023
evidence.summary: Verified Figure 13/f. 165v on p. 114, Figure 14/f. 166r on p. 116, Figure 15/f. 166v on p. 118, the separate semi-diplomatic transcription pages 115, 117, and 119, the final-line opening of CME302 on f. 166r, continuation across f. 166v, the October 2023 running issue designation, and the PDF's 24-page extent. The exact cached file has SHA-256 2d629b3c089186a95ee7795d73f18d1b808d579f93122e03c0aac938dfad903a.

evidence.citation: John Young and P. Henderson Aitken, A Catalogue of the Manuscripts in the Library of the Hunterian Museum in the University of Glasgow (Glasgow, 1908), 245-246
evidence.summary: Historical detailed catalog entry for Hunter 307, including support, extent, ruling, decoration, condition, contents, and former shelfmarks. Its date and binding statements are retained as legacy assertions.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive scan of Young and Aitken's 1908 Hunterian manuscript catalog, identifier catalogueofmanus00hunt
evidence.summary: The complete 594-page PDF was downloaded successfully and maps Hunter 307 to PDF pp. 267-268. Fixity is SHA-256 ff2428018b5543fd1391ee5461f221cab6607fc48b5a002dace163da769acc41.

evidence.citation: University of Glasgow Library, Archives and Special Collections publishing requests and permission fee schedule
evidence.summary: Current instructions require advance permission to publish collection material, acknowledgement, possible fees, and separate clearance of third-party copyright; the linked fee schedule describes circumstances in which permission fees may be waived.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Royal MS 18 A VI, record 040-002107422
evidence.locator: ff. 54r-55v; parallel component ff. 55r-55v per CME302 XML
evidence.summary: Confirms the extant paper-and-parchment medical codex, broad fifteenth-sixteenth-century codex date, and Guy de Chauliac Middle English text on hematoscopy, scarifying and cupping on ff. 54r-55v. Its detailed contents/related-material description identifies that article as another late-fifteenth-century hand. The digitised-content field is blank.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Sloane MS 965, record 040-002113317
evidence.locator: bloodletting tracts ff. 156v-164v; parallel component ff. 163v-164v per CME302 XML
evidence.summary: Confirms the extant codex and dates the English bloodletting tracts on ff. 156v-164v to the fourteenth-fifteenth century; the U-M parallel on ff. 163v-164v lies within that section. The digitised-content field is blank. The top-level 1700-1701 date belongs to a seed-list component and is not applied to the medieval passage.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Sloane MS 3486, record 040-002115873
evidence.locator: CME302 XML literally gives ff. 147v-48; represented as ff. 147v-148 with terminal side unresolved
evidence.summary: Confirms the extant composite medical codex, fifteenth-century English medical contents surrounding the cited locus, and blank digitised-content field. The exact parallel's second- or third-quarter fifteenth-century date is supplied by the 2023 article rather than the record's broad thirteenth-seventeenth-century codex range; no reviewed source resolves whether the XML's terminal f. 148 means recto, verso, or an unspecified part of the folio.

evidence.citation: British Library, Digitised manuscripts and archives
evidence.summary: The current page explains that digitised availability is recorded through catalog discovery and that some legacy digitised content remains unavailable following the cyberattack. The exact three catalog records have no item-level digitised-content link, so no complete public facsimile is asserted.

evidence.citation: British Library Digitisation Services, online order form, and current digitisation and reproduction services price list
evidence.summary: The current beta order route accepts individuals, academics, and researchers; requires shelfmark, title, exact recto/verso folios, format, and payment details; distinguishes reference images from reproduction-quality photography; and says original manuscripts are photographed rather than scanned. The two-page GBP price list is based on 2025/26 rates. Ordering does not itself grant reproduction rights.

evidence.citation: British Library, Imaging Services terms and conditions, updated January 2026
evidence.summary: The January 2026 terms say intellectual-property clearance remains the requester's responsibility and prohibit copying or reproducing supplied material without the Library's written permission, which may carry additional fees. They direct permissions enquiries to permissions@bl.uk; the service page separately gives licensing@bl.uk for image reuse.

open_questions.question: Does VK 1841 represent an independently circulating garsing work, a version-specific component of On Bloodletting, or both under different witness boundaries?
next_steps: Consult the complete current eVK2 records for v0034490000, v0057530000, v0088010000, and v0096920000.
Compare the opening, closing, and surrounding material in all four witnesses.
Keep the three British Library witnesses as cited collateral versions rather than ancestors until direct collation establishes their relationship.

open_questions.question: Will Glasgow and the WebHunter rights holders authorize a complete private research cache or publication/repository reuse of the Hunter 307 image sequence?
next_steps: Request written permission from Glasgow Archives and Special Collections and ask which body controls the WebHunter derivatives.
Ask whether private bulk research storage, scholarly quotation, noncommercial repository display, and commercial print-on-demand reuse are treated differently.
If permission is granted, construct an overlap-aware full sequence rather than concatenating all three editorial segments.
notes: No image bundle was cached under the current express no-copy wording.

open_questions.question: Do complete or work-covering digital surrogates exist for Royal MS 18 A VI, Sloane MS 965, or Sloane MS 3486 outside the current public catalog records?
next_steps: Ask the British Library whether legacy photography, microfilm, or restored digital objects cover the exact folios.
Request whole-codex or work-covering reproductions and item-level reuse terms if no public object exists.
Recheck the exact catalog records as post-cyberattack services are restored.

open_questions.question: How should the 2023 article's claim to provide the first edition of the witness be reconciled with Schaffner's 2020 transcript and the U-M item's 2023 publication date?
next_steps: Establish the exact public release date of U-M CME302 and whether the article authors had access to it before publication.
Distinguish a raw digital transcript from a formally published semi-diplomatic scholarly edition in any future priority statement.

open_questions.question: Which side or extent of Sloane MS 3486, f. 148 completes the U-M XML's literal locator 'ff. 147v-48'?
next_steps: Consult the complete eVK2 v0096920000 record or obtain images of ff. 147v-148v.
Ask the British Library to confirm the exact terminal side when ordering photography.
Retain ff. 147v-148 without silently expanding it to 148r until direct evidence resolves the range.
